---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# OPTIMIZAÇÃO — O1: Maximizar lucro semanal 
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# ── Parâmetros do modelo de negócio ─────────────────────────────────────────
STORE_PARAMS <- list(
  baltimore    = list(FJ=1.00, FX=1.15, W=700),
  lancaster    = list(FJ=1.05, FX=1.20, W=730),
  philadelphia = list(FJ=1.10, FX=1.15, W=760),
  richmond     = list(FJ=1.15, FX=1.25, W=800)
)

# Custos HR por worker por dia
HR_COST <- list(
  X_weekday = 80,
  X_weekend = 95,
  J_weekday = 60,
  J_weekend = 70
)

# Custos fixos semanais por loja
STORE_FIXED_COST <- list(
  baltimore    = 700,
  lancaster    = 730,   
  philadelphia = 760,
  richmond     = 800    
)

CAP_X      <- 7
CAP_J      <- 6
IS_WEEKEND <- c(TRUE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, TRUE)


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FUNÇÃO DE AVALIAÇÃO — eval_s1
# s = c(J_1..J_7, X_1..X_7, PR_1..PR_7) — vector de 21 valores
# pred = vector de 7 valores (Num_Customers previstos para cada dia)
# store = nome da loja (para buscar W_s)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
eval_s1 <- function(s, pred, store = "baltimore") {

  params <- STORE_PARAMS[[store]]
  FJ     <- params$FJ
  FX     <- params$FX
  W_s    <- params$W

  J  <- round(s[1:7])
  X  <- round(s[8:14])
  PR <- pmax(0, pmin(0.30, s[15:21]))

  weekly_return <- 0

  for (d in 1:7) {
    C_d  <- pred[d]
    X_d  <- X[d]
    J_d  <- J[d]
    PR_d <- PR[d]

    by_X <- min(X_d * CAP_X, C_d)
    by_J <- min(J_d * CAP_J, max(0, C_d - by_X))

    log_term <- log(2 - PR_d)
    U_X      <- round(FX * 10 / log_term)
    U_J      <- round(FJ * 10 / log_term)

    Xunits <- by_X * U_X
    Junits <- by_J * U_J

    Xsales <- round(Xunits * (1 - PR_d) * 1.07)
    Jsales <- round(Junits * (1 - PR_d) * 1.07)

    if (IS_WEEKEND[d]) {
      XHRc <- X_d * HR_COST$X_weekend
      JHRc <- J_d * HR_COST$J_weekend
    } else {
      XHRc <- X_d * HR_COST$X_weekday
      JHRc <- J_d * HR_COST$J_weekday
    }

    R_d           <- Xsales + Jsales - XHRc - JHRc
    weekly_return <- weekly_return + R_d
  }

  Rs <- weekly_return - W_s
  return(Rs)
}


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# VERIFICAÇÃO — reproduzir o exemplo do slide (Baltimore → lucro = 146)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
verify_eval <- function() {
  cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════\n")
  cat("VERIFICAÇÃO DA FUNÇÃO eval_s1\n")
  cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

  # Dados reais da semana de exemplo (slide imagem 2)
  pred_verify <- c(97, 61, 65, 71, 65, 89, 125)  # Num_Customers reais

  # Plano do slide (PR, X, J por dia su→sa)
  J_verify  <- c(0,  10, 4, 0,  5, 5, 4)
  X_verify  <- c(4,  0,  8, 20, 0, 4, 3)
  PR_verify <- c(0.00, 0.05, 0.10, 0.15, 0.20, 0.25, 0.30)

  s_verify <- c(J_verify, X_verify, PR_verify)

  # Cálculo detalhado para debug
  cat("Dia | Clientes | by_X | by_J | Xunits | Junits | Xsales | Jsales | XHRc | JHRc | R_d\n")
  cat(paste(rep("-", 100), collapse=""), "\n")

  for (d in 1:7) {
    day_names <- c("su","mo","tu","we","th","fr","sa")
    C_d  <- pred_verify[d]
    X_d  <- X_verify[d]
    J_d  <- J_verify[d]
    PR_d <- PR_verify[d]

    by_X <- min(X_d * CAP_X, C_d)
    by_J <- min(J_d * CAP_J, max(0, C_d - by_X))

    log_term <- log(2 - PR_d)
    U_X      <- round(FX * 10 / log_term)
    U_J      <- round(FJ * 10 / log_term)

    Xunits <- by_X * U_X
    Junits <- by_J * U_J

    Xsales <- round(Xunits * (1 - PR_d) * 1.07)
    Jsales <- round(Junits * (1 - PR_d) * 1.07)

    if (IS_WEEKEND[d]) {
      XHRc <- X_d * HR_COST$X_weekend
      JHRc <- J_d * HR_COST$J_weekend
    } else {
      XHRc <- X_d * HR_COST$X_weekday
      JHRc <- J_d * HR_COST$J_weekday
    }

    R_d <- Xsales + Jsales - XHRc - JHRc

    cat(sprintf(" %s  | %8d | %4d | %4d | %6d | %6d | %6d | %6d | %4d | %4d | %4d\n",
                day_names[d], C_d, by_X, by_J,
                Xunits, Junits, Xsales, Jsales, XHRc, JHRc, R_d))
  }

  result     <- eval_s1(s_verify, pred_verify, store="baltimore")
  expected   <- 146

  cat(paste(rep("-", 100), collapse=""), "\n")
  cat(sprintf("\nLucro semanal calculado : %d\n", result))
  cat(sprintf("Lucro esperado (slide)  : %d\n", expected))

  if (result == expected) {
    cat("✓ VERIFICAÇÃO PASSOU — eval_s1 está correctamente implementada!\n")
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("✗ VERIFICAÇÃO FALHOU — diferença de %d\n", result - expected))
  }
  cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════\n")
}

verify_eval()
```


```{r}
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# MONTE CARLO — O1: Maximizar lucro semanal (Baltimore, n=1000)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Previsões para a próxima semana (vêm do modelo de forecasting)
# Substitui pelos valores reais do prev_arimax_balt ou prev_balt

pred_balt <- c(102,87,83,88,94,89,92)   # 7 valores previstos

# ── Bounds da solução ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# s = c(J_1..J_7, X_1..X_7, PR_1..PR_7)
lower_balt <- rep(0, 21)
upper_balt <- c(
  ceiling(pred_balt / CAP_J),   # máx J workers por dia
  ceiling(pred_balt / CAP_X),   # máx X workers por dia
  rep(0.30, 7)                  # máx PR por dia
)

cat("\n═══════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("OPTIMIZAÇÃO O1 — Baltimore\n")
cat("Previsões próximos 7 dias:", pred_balt, "\n")
cat("Lower bounds:", lower_balt, "\n")
cat("Upper bounds:", round(upper_balt, 2), "\n")
cat("═══════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

# ── Monte Carlo — n=1000 soluções aleatórias ─────────────────────────────────
set.seed(42)
N_MC <- 1000

best_profit <- -Inf
best_s      <- NULL

profits_mc  <- numeric(N_MC)

for (i in 1:N_MC) {
  # Gerar solução aleatória dentro dos bounds
  s_rand <- numeric(21)

  # J e X: inteiros aleatórios
  for (k in 1:14) {
    s_rand[k] <- sample(lower_balt[k]:upper_balt[k], 1)
  }

  # PR: valores reais aleatórios [0, 0.30]
  s_rand[15:21] <- runif(7, min=0, max=0.30)

  # Avaliar
  profit       <- eval_s1(s_rand, pred_balt, store="baltimore")
  profits_mc[i] <- profit

  # Guardar melhor
  if (profit > best_profit) {
    best_profit <- profit
    best_s      <- s_rand
  }
}
```


```{r}
# ── Resultados ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("── Resultados Monte Carlo O1 (n=1000) ──\n")
cat("Melhor lucro encontrado :", round(best_profit, 2), "$\n")
cat("Mediana dos lucros      :", round(median(profits_mc), 2), "$\n")
cat("Média dos lucros        :", round(mean(profits_mc), 2), "$\n")
cat("Lucro mínimo            :", round(min(profits_mc), 2), "$\n\n")

# ── Melhor plano encontrado ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
best_J  <- round(best_s[1:7])
best_X  <- round(best_s[8:14])
best_PR <- round(best_s[15:21], 2)

day_names <- c("su","mo","tu","we","th","fr","sa")

cat("── Melhor plano semanal ──\n")
cat(sprintf("%-6s", "day:"),  paste(sprintf("%6s", day_names),  collapse=""), "\n")
cat(sprintf("%-6s", "PR:"),   paste(sprintf("%6.2f", best_PR),  collapse=""), "\n")
cat(sprintf("%-6s", "HR X:"), paste(sprintf("%6d", best_X),     collapse=""), "\n")
cat(sprintf("%-6s", "HR J:"), paste(sprintf("%6d", best_J),     collapse=""), "\n")
cat(sprintf("week profit = %d\n", best_profit))

# ── Histograma da distribuição dos lucros ────────────────────────────────────
hist(profits_mc,
     breaks = 50,
     main   = "Baltimore — Distribuição dos lucros (Monte Carlo O1, n=1000)",
     xlab   = "Lucro semanal ($)",
     ylab   = "Frequência",
     col    = "steelblue",
     border = "white")
abline(v = best_profit, col = "red",    lty = 2, lwd = 2)
abline(v = median(profits_mc), col = "orange", lty = 2, lwd = 2)
legend("topright",
       c(paste0("Melhor=", round(best_profit)),
         paste0("Mediana=", round(median(profits_mc)))),
       col = c("red","orange"), lty = 2, lwd = 2)
```
